name: Strategy
description: Define a family of algorithms, encapsulate each one, and make them interchangeable. Strategy lets the algorithm vary independently from clients that use it.
problem: Need to use different variants of an algorithm within an object and switch between them at runtime.
solution: Extract all algorithms into separate classes called strategies and let the context delegate the work to a strategy object.
when_to_use: When you have many related classes that differ only in their behavior
When you need different variants of an algorithm
When an algorithm uses data clients shouldn't know about
When a class defines many behaviors via conditional statements
when_not_to_use: When there are only a few algorithms that rarely change
When clients must be aware of the differences between strategies

participants.name: Strategy
participants.role: Declares interface common to all supported algorithms

participants.name: ConcreteStrategy
participants.role: Implements the algorithm using the Strategy interface

participants.name: Context
participants.role: Maintains a reference to a Strategy object, may define interface for Strategy to access its data

pros: Algorithms can be swapped at runtime
cons: Clients must be aware of different strategies
